claim: The severity of cardiac involvement in amyloidosis can be described by the degree of transmurality of late gadolinium enhancement in MRI.

result.claim: The severity of cardiac involvement in amyloidosis can be described by the degree of transmurality of late gadolinium enhancement in MRI.
result.reasoning: The claim is strongly supported by evidence from paper ID 13768432, "Prognostic Value of Late Gadolinium Enhancement Cardiovascular Magnetic Resonance in Cardiac Amyloidosis." The abstract of this paper explicitly states that "Transmural LGE is determined reliably by PSIR and represents advanced cardiac amyloidosis." It further elaborates that LGE patterns (none, subendocardial, and transmural) were associated with increasing amyloid burden, as defined by extracellular volume (P<0.0001). This directly links the degree of transmurality of late gadolinium enhancement (LGE) in MRI to the severity of cardiac involvement in amyloidosis. Additionally, the paper highlights the prognostic value of transmural LGE, showing it predicted death independently of other known prognostic factors, further underscoring its significance in describing disease severity.

evidence.text: 119 patients had AL amyloidosis.
evidence.source: Prognostic Value of Late Gadolinium Enhancement Cardiovascular Magnetic Resonance in Cardiac Amyloidosis

evidence.text: Patients with diabetes mellitus are known to have a high morbidity and mortality after an acute myocardial infarction.
evidence.source: Impact of diabetes on long-term prognosis in patients with unstable angina and non-Q-wave myocardial infarction: results of the OASIS (Organization to Assess Strategies for Ischemic Syndromes) Registry.

evidence.text: Diabetes mellitus is a major risk factor for adverse outcomes after acute coronary syndromes.
evidence.source: Platelet glycoprotein IIb/IIIa inhibitors reduce mortality in diabetic patients with non-ST-segment-elevation acute coronary syndromes.

evidence.text: Patients with diabetes who have unstable angina or non-Q-wave myocardial infarction are especially likely to experience high 2-year morbidity and mortality.
evidence.source: Impact of diabetes on long-term prognosis in patients with unstable angina and non-Q-wave myocardial infarction: results of the OASIS (Organization to Assess Strategies for Ischemic Syndromes) Registry.

evidence.text: IFN-gamma receptor-deficient mice (IFN-gammaR(-/-)) on a BALB/c background immunized with a peptide derived from cardiac alpha-myosin heavy chain develop severe myocarditis.
evidence.source: Lethal autoimmune myocarditis in interferon-gamma receptor-deficient mice: enhanced disease severity by impaired inducible nitric oxide synthase induction.

evidence.text: Synthetic associations in the sickle cell anemia case extended over a 2.5-Mb interval.
evidence.source: Rare Variants Create Synthetic Genome-Wide Associations

evidence.text: The damaged myocardium receiving viable 3DFC exhibited a significantly greater angiogenic response than nonviable and untreated control groups 14 and 30 days after surgery.
evidence.source: Scaffold-based three-dimensional human fibroblast culture provides a structural matrix that supports angiogenesis in infarcted heart tissue.

evidence.text: Disorders of lysine acetylation may be correlated with obesity, diabetes, and cardiovascular disease.
evidence.source: Protein Lysine Acetylated/Deacetylated Enzymes and the Metabolism-Related Diseases

evidence.text: Plasma concentrations of apolipoprotein AI were significantly higher in patients with occluded grafts (p less than 0.04).
evidence.source: Influence of smoking and plasma factors on patency of femoropopliteal vein grafts.

evidence.text: Mutations that raise LDLRs reduce LDL and diminish heart attacks.
evidence.source: A Century of Cholesterol and Coronaries: From Plaques to Genes to Statins

evidence.text: Genome-wide association studies are identifying disease-associated chromosome regions.
evidence.source: Large-scale genetic fine mapping and genotype-phenotype associations implicate polymorphism in the IL2RA region in type 1 diabetes

evidence.text: IL-12/IFN-gamma (Th1) axis was investigated in the development of autoimmune myocarditis.

evidence.text: The prognosis and treatment of AL and ATTR amyloidosis are substantially influenced by cardiac involvement.
evidence.source: Prognostic Value of Late Gadolinium Enhancement Cardiovascular Magnetic Resonance in Cardiac Amyloidosis

evidence.text: The glomerular filtration rate (GFR) is especially dependent on Ang II during stenosis of a dominant or single kidney.
evidence.source: Renal considerations in angiotensin converting enzyme inhibitor therapy: a statement for healthcare professionals from the Council on the Kidney in Cardiovascular Disease and the Council for High Blood Pressure Research of the American Heart Association.

evidence.text: GFR is highly angiotensin II (Ang II) dependent during high-grade bilateral renal artery stenosis.

evidence.text: Cardiovascular magnetic resonance with late gadolinium enhancement (LGE) is a reference standard for the diagnosis of cardiac amyloidosis.
